title: [phase:critique] [family:kernel-policy] [provider_hint:codex] Review the intended policy change: Kernel should not return BLOCKED immediately when Claude/GLM/lanes are missing; it should first attempt fallback reviewer models, replacement lanes, and concrete unblock actions so development keeps movi...
summary_text: 1. [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md:117) `Strictness by mode` currently encodes fail-closed on missing Claude/GLM; this is the primary SSOT policy text to change. 2. [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/CODEX.md:69) Adapter-level rule repeats fail-closed wording for missing Claude/GLM in strict `/vote`. 3. [.codex/prompts/kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:33) and [.codex/prompts/k.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.codex/prompts/k.md:36) Kernel bootstrap/preflight currently allows immediate `BLOCKED` outcomes; this is where “attempt replacement lanes + unblock actions first” should be explicit. 4. [scripts/lib/shadow-continuity-policy.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/lib/shadow-continuity-policy.sh:145) Baseline trio gate logic hard-fails when direct Claude/GLM are missing even if fallback lanes succeeded. 5. [scripts/harness/aggregate-tutti-votes.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/aggregate-tutti-votes.sh:177) Consumes that gate and forces `weighted_vote_passed=false` when baseline fails, which is where fallback/remediation-first behavior must be reflected. 6. [scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/run-kernel-council-review.sh:326) Terminal hard-stop (`exit 1`) on baseline/execute failure; likely needs a remediation/replacement attempt stage before final fail. 7. [scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh:143) Static contract test pins current `BLOCKED` wording; update this guard if prompt language changes. Note: fallback execution attempts already exist in [subscription-agent-runner.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/harness/subscription-agent-runner.sh:928). The gap is mainly policy gating/adjudication after those attempts. Also, relaxing immediate `BLOCKED` should not permit single-lane or Codex-only completion.
